# Basic Markdown

Simple document with h1 and h2 headers, no code blocks.
This document is intentionally longer to test proper chunking behavior.

## Getting Started

This is a getting started section that explains the basics of the framework.
It covers installation, setup, and configuration in great detail. Users should
read this section carefully before proceeding to more advanced topics. The
framework is designed to be easy to use while providing powerful features for
building modern applications. It supports multiple deployment targets and can
be extended with plugins and middleware for additional functionality.

## Installation

Install the framework via npm or bun. Choose your preferred package manager.
The installation process is straightforward and should only take a few seconds.
After installation, you can verify the setup by running the included test suite.
Make sure you have the required dependencies installed on your system before
proceeding. The framework supports Node.js 18+ and Bun 1.0+.

## Configuration

Configuration is done via a config file or environment variables. You can
customize every aspect of the framework to fit your specific needs. The config
file supports JSON, YAML, and TypeScript formats. Environment variables take
precedence over config file values, allowing for flexible deployment scenarios.
Default values are provided for all options, so you only need to configure
what you want to change from the defaults.
